description_long: This apartment building complex consists of two main structures: a taller high-rise and a shorter mid-rise, both connected by a shared street and sidewalk. The taller building features multiple floors with a regular grid of windows and several balconies, while the shorter building has a more compact form with a flat roof and fewer stories. The exterior is finished in a combination of light and dark tones, with blue window frames and balcony railings adding visual contrast. The street between the buildings is lined with small trees and paved walkways, suggesting a residential urban environment. The buildings have flat roofs, some of which appear accessible, and the facades are characterized by clean lines and modern materials. The overall design is functional and contemporary, with an emphasis on geometric forms and efficient use of space.
description: A modern apartment building complex with two connected structures, featuring flat roofs, regular window patterns, and blue accents. The design is contemporary, with clean lines and a mix of light and dark exterior finishes.
description_view_0: Side view showing both the taller and shorter buildings, the connecting street, and sidewalk with trees.
description_view_1: Rear view of the taller building, highlighting the flat roof and blue-accented windows and balconies.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the full height of the taller building and the street running between the two structures.
description_view_3: Front view of the taller building, displaying the main facade with balconies, large windows, and the entrance area.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a regular, grid-like pattern with consistent spacing and alignment across all floors, contributing to a symmetrical and orderly facade.
